channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Autorregulación, la ruta de las Sofomes para fortalecer la ciberseguridad
item.description: Las cerca de 2,000 Sociedades Financieras de Objeto Múltiple (Sofomes) que operan en México, especializadas en otorgar créditos a sectores y nichos no siempre atendidos por otras entidades financieras, decidieron autorregularse en materia de ciberseguridad. “Adoptamos esquemas de autorregulación tanto en materia de estados financieros como de ciberseguridad. Como no están sujetas a los mismos estándares regulatorios que la banca, la Asociación de Sofomes (Asofom) impulsó ese camino para adoptar las mejores prácticas.

item.title: CNBV: la digitalización financiera se acelera, pero hay más riesgos en ciberseguridad
item.description: Durante su participación en el FinSeg Day 2026, organizado por iupana en Ciudad de México, el directivo destacó que la CNBV ha identificado riesgos relevantes asociados a la protección de los datos y a la continuidad operativa de las instituciones. Pero el regulador detectó una modalidad más compleja denominada “triple extorsión” , una amenaza que ha cobrado auge a nivel global. Entre ellos, destaca el ransomware, un tipo de intrusión para secuestrar la información a cambio de un rescate, así como ataques dirigidos a servidores con el objetivo de interrumpir servicios.

item.title: Florida Needs More – Much More – Wind Mitigation, Say Experts at OIR Summit
item.description: Florida has the largest wind-mitigation grant program in the country, providing more than $300 million to homeowners over the last few years and resulting in insurance premium discounts for thousands of people. That was the word from Florida professors, actuaries and insurance interests who participated in a panel discussion at the Florida Office of Insurance Regulation’s Insurance Summit, held last week in Tallahassee. “If investing in upgrades adds as much value as you put into the upgrade, why aren’t all developers doing it?” asked session attendee Mark Tanner, an actuary with Insurance Strategies Consulting.

item.title: Meta to Capture Employee Mouse Moves, Keystrokes to Train AI: Reuters
item.description: Meta is installing new tracking software on U.S.-based employees’ computers to capture mouse movements, clicks and keystrokes for use in training its artificial-intelligence models, part of a broad initiative to build AI agents that can perform work tasks autonomously, the company told staffers in internal memos seen by Reuters. To help, we’re launching an internal tool that will capture these kinds of inputs on certain applications to help us train our models,” said Stone. The tool will run on a list of work-related apps and websites and will also take occasional snapshots of the content on employees’ screens for context, according to one memo, posted by a staff AI research scientist on Tuesday in a dedicated internal channel for the company’s model-building Meta SuperIntelligence Labs team.

item.title: With 85% of Lloyd’s business global, London seeks international access in the face of new regulatory regimes
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Fundamentals’ driving APAC’s asset-intensive reinsurance growth, with ample runway ahead: Aon
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Willis appoints Scott Kirkwood as head of natural resources for Pacific
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: California Insurance Commissioner Race Has Diverse Field, With ‘Insurance Crisis’ at Center of Platforms
item.description: The race for the California Insurance Commissioner post has drawn 11 candidates covering several parties, and backgrounds that include two insurance agents, a former broker, a state legislator and a former state legislator. Addressing the state’s “insurance crisis,” which took on a bigger priority following the devastating January 2025 Los Angles Wildfires, is a loud message coming from most of the field. Related: California Commissioner Advances Proposal to Overhaul Intervenor Process Incumbent Democratic Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara, who is term-limited after serving two terms, has not made known any next steps in his political career.

item.title: Allied Trust selects BriteCore
item.description: Allied Trust Insurance has gone live on the platform from BriteCore , replacing legacy systems with a single core system covering policy, billing, and claims. The move consolidates operations into one platform, allowing Allied Trust to manage its business through a unified workflow while reducing reliance on fragmented systems. For Allied Trust, the shift is tied to growth.

item.title: Too Tipsy to Drive? German Suppliers Have In-Car Sensor for That
item.description: Two German suppliers have come up with in-built sensors to test a driver’s blood alcohol level from a fingertip, delivering results in seconds using a touchscreen sensor and AI-based analysis. When a finger touches the sensor, invisible light pulses out and reaches the tissue under the fingertip and any ethanol molecules there. The light that reflects back to the sensor is evaluated by AI-based methods to assess the alcohol content in the blood, according to a statement.

item.title: Lawsuit Accuses JetBlue of Using Personal Customer Data to Raise Fares
item.description: According to a complaint filed late Wednesday in Brooklyn federal court, JetBlue conceals its use of “trackers” to set prices dynamically, and shares data with third parties whose programs help it decide when to raise fares. We’re sorry for your loss.” The Long Island City, New York-based carrier said on Monday its response was incorrect, while adding that “fares can change at any moment as seats are purchased or as inventory is adjusted based on demand.” On Tuesday, two Democratic lawmakers in Congress asked JetBlue to respond to detailed questions about pricing, including whether it uses personal data “to inform prices.” In November, two dozen congressional lawmakers asked Delta Air Lines to address whether it used or plans to use generative AI in setting prices. JetBlue has been sued in a proposed class action claiming it uses customers’ personal data to set ticket prices, after its response to a social media post raised concern that the carrier employed “surveillance pricing” to make flying more expensive.

item.title: Jaguar Land Rover Recalling 170,000 US Vehicles Over Loss of Drive Power
item.description: Related: Ford Recalling 140,000 US Vehicles Over Damaged Wires A failure of the DC-DC converter will stop the vehicle’s 12-Volt system from charging and can lead to complete loss of drive power and exterior lighting, the auto regulator said. Jaguar Land Rover is recalling 170,169 vehicles in the U.S. The recall affects multiple models including Range Rover, Discovery, Range Rover Sport, Defender, etc., NHTSA said.

item.title: Potential for Loss Motivates Employees More Than Possible Gain, Study Shows
item.description: A Virginia Tech researcher and his colleagues discovered that when managers frame work problems as a potential loss, employees are more likely to take action than when those problems are framed as potential gains. The research also revealed that when the potential loss impacts a larger group, employees are more likely to take action in the form of speaking up to a supervisor in hopes of finding a solution. For managers, this research suggests that framing work problems as potential losses can influence employees to speak up more.

item.title: 2026 Viral Work Trends
item.description: Each year brings new viral job search trends, and 2026 is no different. Even in these early months, I have already noticed trends that, while not exclusive to the insurance industry, could be critical to help you in recruitment: This term has become increasingly popular on social media platforms. Some third-party recruiters offer a pay-for-work model.

item.title: CEOs in Their 60s Are New Norm With Companies Picking Older Bosses
item.description: This graying of the corner office, shown in a National Bureau of Economic Research working paper that analyzed the hiring and career patterns of more than 50,500 CEOs in the US between 2000 and 2023, reflects “fundamental changes” in how executives build careers and how companies select their leaders, according to the study’s authors, with varied business experience favored over raw ability. “As executives require longer career paths to build such diverse capabilities, firms appoint older CEOs.” The trend is most pronounced among smaller, privately-held firms, the researchers said. That’s because larger companies can offer internal candidates a wider range of assignments, cultivating a well-roundedness that smaller companies “can only access by hiring executives who accumulated that experience by moving across many firms and industries.” (This also helps explain why the average age of CEOs at S&P 500 Index companies is below the average and not advancing as fast, rising from roughly 56 years old in 2000 to 58.5 in 2023.) Executives with experience in the strategy consulting sector — which “fast-tracks the accumulation of generalist skills,” the researchers note — have grabbed a greater share of CEO roles , according to a separate analysis by Live Data Technologies for Bloomberg.

item.title: Why hurricane peak uncertainty offers a strategic window for cat bond entry: Powell, Brookmont
item.description: While the broader catastrophe bond market often adopts a wait-and-see posture until the Atlantic hurricane season passes, the current 2026 landscape suggests that the most compelling value may be found during this window of peak uncertainty, according to Ethan Powell, Principal & Chief Investment Officer of Brookmont Capital Management, LLC. As the upcoming season is poised to commence in just a few weeks, Powell recently discussed with Artemis the outlook that Brookmont Capital anticipates for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, as well as its implications for the cat bond market. However, as Powell stresses, the current pre-season window, with its higher uncertainty and potential for higher yields is actually a strategic time to allocate.

item.title: American Coastal secures targeted $200m Florida multi-peril cover with Armor Re II 2026-1 cat bond
item.description: As we’ve previously explained, this Armor Re II Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond marks the first Florida-focused multi-peril cat bond where American Coastal Insurance Company is the sole sponsor. American Coastal Insurance Company (AmCoastal Insurance) has now successfully priced its latest catastrophe bond, securing the targeted $200 million multi-peril reinsurance from the capital markets from its Armor Re II Ltd. Now priced, the confirmed $200 million Armor Re II 2026-1 cat bond will provide American Coastal with a just over three-year source of Florida focused reinsurance protection, for losses from the perils of named storm, earthquake fire following, severe weather and fire.

item.title: Schroders Capital & Hannover Re integrate tokenisation capability into ILS platform
item.description: Schroders Capital’s Private Debt & Credit Alternatives (PDCA) team, together with global reinsurer Hannover Re, have unveiled the integration of an innovative tokenised capability into its insurance-linked securities (ILS) investment platform, which includes the execution of the first real-world collateralized reinsurance transaction through this platform. This model also builds on Schroders Capital’s pilot tokenisation project that was launched in 2024, which the firm also partnered on with Hannover Re, which was designed to enhance the way ILS assets are invested and managed. Schroders Capital also said that the launch of this new tokenised ILS capability represents the next phase of its commitment to innovating investment solutions for the benefit of the firm’s clients.

item.title: Digital insurance growth in Africa and the Middle East highlights reinsurance capacity gaps, EIRS warns
item.description: Looking beyond Africa, EIRS highlights that insurance penetration across both Africa and the Middle East has largely remained below 3%, which it attributes not to weak demand but to structural limitations in risk capacity, distribution reach, and reinsurance depth. EIRS, a company working on digital insurance infrastructure and reinsurance solutions across emerging markets, says the rapid expansion of digital insurance in Africa and the Middle East is exposing a structural imbalance in how risk is supported and transferred. The company highlights that in several African markets, including Kenya and South Africa, motor insurance remains a key part of the industry but is increasingly challenged by rising claims costs, fraud exposure, and competitive pricing.

item.title: Chubb CEO highlights misalignment between property supply-demand factors
item.description: During global insurer Chubb’s first quarter earnings call, CEO Evan Greenberg described the rapid softening in the property insurance market as “dumb,” citing a misalignment between supply-demand factors related to capital in the re/insurance industry and high intermediation costs. Chubb reported that it reduced exposures in its Major Accounts and Excess and Surplus (E&S) divisions by non-renewing a substantial portion of shared and layered property insurance business in Q1. In addition to walking away from business it deemed inadequately priced, Chubb also purchased additional reinsurance to further protect its remaining property risks.

item.title: EU insurers and reinsurers expand private credit exposure: S&P
item.description: S&P explains that larger global insurers and reinsurers began developing private credit capabilities more than a decade ago, while smaller regional firms entered the market later and often rely on external asset managers to gain exposure. S&P Global Ratings, the credit rating agency, reports that insurers and reinsurers in the European Union have been steadily increasing their allocation to private credit. S&P Global Ratings states that EU-based re and insurers have raised their exposure to private credit to 5.8% in the second quarter of 2025 from 3.9% in the fourth quarter of 2016.

item.title: Labor Department Unveils Proposal on Contract, Franchise Worker Pay
item.description: Department of Labor unveiled a proposed rule that would narrow the circumstances in which a business can be considered the “joint employer” of another company’s workers under the federal law requiring a minimum wage and overtime pay. Acting Labor Secretary Keith Sonderling said the goal of the proposal was to make it easier for employers to comply with the law, ultimately benefiting workers. The proposal will be formally published on Thursday and the department will collect public comments for 60 days.

item.title: Iran Shows Off its Control Over Strait After Collapse of Peace Talks
item.description: Iran flaunted its tightened grip over the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday with video of its commandos storming a huge cargo ship, after the collapse of peace talks that Washington had hoped would open the world’s most important shipping corridor. President Donald Trump said it was Washington that was in “total control” of the strait, which he described as “‘Sealed up Tight,’ until such time as Iran is able to make a DEAL!!!.” Trump and his military officials have said Iran’s navy is “at the bottom of the sea,” but Tehran’s speedboats show that it can still wreak havoc on shipping. Iran said it had captured both on Wednesday, accusing them of trying to cross the strait without permits.

item.title: Amwins Partnering With Vivere on California FAIR Plan Wrap Product
item.description: Amwins and Vivere have partnered to launch a California FAIR Plan Wrap product. Developed by Vivere and distributed through Amwins, the product offers comprehensive coverage and fast quotes to the entire portfolios of California FAIR plan insureds, including commercial and dwelling risks. Related: California Wildfire Risk Bills Cruising Through Legislature The new wrap product was developed to complement the California FAIR Plan with Using only a completed California FAIR Plan application, retailers can generate quotes for an individual risk or entire portfolios of FAIR Plan insureds within minutes.

item.title: Cytora and LexisNexis Risk Solutions announce strategic relationship to enhance risk selection and automation for U.S. commercial insurers
item.description: The post Cytora and LexisNexis Risk Solutions announce strategic relationship to enhance risk selection and automation for U.S. Cytora and LexisNexis Risk Solutions have announced a strategic relationship to enhance risk selection and automation for U.S. commercial and life insurance at LexisNexis Risk Solutions, said: “Working with Cytora represents a strategic leap forward, specifically benefitting U.S.

item.title: Liberty Mutual partners with Ethos to launch fully digital life insurance experience
item.description: We built Ethos to make that possible, and Liberty Mutual’s commitment to their customers makes them perfectly suited to bring that vision to even more families.” The post Liberty Mutual partners with Ethos to launch fully digital life insurance experience appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Ethos Technologies has partnered with Liberty Mutual to broaden access to Liberty Mutual’s direct-to-consumer life insurance offering through a fully digital experience. Ethos has built the kind of platform that lets us deliver on that instant decisions, no unnecessary friction, and coverage options that fit people’s lives.” Chris Fioriotto, Senior Vice President of Insurance at Ethos, added: “Liberty Mutual is one of the most recognized and trusted insurance brands in the country, and we couldn’t be more proud to power their life insurance experience.

item.title: ING launches as MGA with Qover as technology partner
item.description: Financial services company ING Group has launched as a managing general agent (MGA), bringing insurance operations in-house with technology from Qover . This was one of the most complex programs we’ve ever delivered – and it’s a genuine validation of what this technology can do at scale.” – Quentin Colmant, CEO & Co-founder of Qover. Qover provides the orchestration layer, connecting multiple insurers and products through a single platform.

item.title: Inszone acquires James R. Vozar Insurance Agency
item.description: Inszone Insurance acquired Michigan based James R. Vozar Insurance Agency, adding three offices in Athens, Quincy, and Union City and strengthening its presence in the Midwest. Founded in 1969, the agency is known for its agricultural and farm insurance expertise, and its leadership team, including Mike Vozar and Brian Earles, will remain through the transition.

item.title: Voltaire Recognized With Multiple Industry Honors, Named 2026 Stevie® Award Winner and PC360 Insurance Luminaries Finalist
item.description: PR Contact: Yo Sub Kwon, CEO Voltaire [pr] @voltaire.claims 332-334-6767 The post Voltaire Recognized With Multiple Industry Honors, Named 2026 Stevie® Award Winner and PC360 Insurance Luminaries Finalist appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . In The 2026 American Business Awards®, Voltaire earned: Gold Stevie® — AI-Driven Startup of the Year Gold Stevie® — Artificial Intelligence / Machine Learning Solution — Generative (audio, graphics, text, video) Silver Stevie® — Insurance Solution Silver Stevie® — Most Innovative Tech Company of the Year — Up to 100 Employees Voltaire was also named a finalist in the 2026 PropertyCasualty360 Insurance Luminaries program, which honors the companies, teams, and executives driving innovation and measurable impact across the insurance industry. Voltaire was named a winner of four Stevie® Awards in the 24 th Annual American Business Awards®, including two Gold Stevies®, and was separately named a finalist in the 2026 PropertyCasualty360 Insurance Luminaries program.

item.title: How Insurers Are Using AI to Manage Rising Catastrophic and Accumulation Risk
item.description: In the U.S., 2025 was the sixth consecutive year in which total catastrophic losses topped $100 billion , and in the UK, insurers paid out a record £6.1 billion in property claims in 2025, with an estimated £1.2 billion of those claims coming from adverse weather events, following catastrophic rainfall and flooding throughout the country. The insurance and reinsurance industry is facing a sustained increase in catastrophic and location‑based risks from extreme weather and natural catastrophes to political violence, terrorism and complex industrial losses.

item.title: PartnerRe hands APAC trio new leadership roles, with regional CEO James Beedle to retire
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA publishes findings from financial adviser market survey
item.description: We have published findings from our Financial Adviser Survey . The findings provide an updated picture of how the UK financial advice market is evolving and what this means for firms, consumers and future growth. The survey brings together responses from more than 4,100 financial advice firms; alongside analysis of data we already hold on around 31,000 advisers.

item.title: FCA calls on law firms and claims management companies to consider the position of their clients
item.description: Any law firm or claims management company (CMC) involved in a potential challenge against the scheme that also has clients making motor finance claims should consider their position and that of their clients carefully. They should give those clients the option of exiting the contract and strongly consider waiving any fees. At the very least, they should write to those clients to explain they’re involved in a challenge that’s likely to delay compensation.

item.title: Sapia agrees to pay more than £19m to WealthTek clients after failing to protect client money
item.description: This resulted in Sapia holding and being responsible for protecting client money resulting from WealthTek’s activities. Sapia has agreed to make a voluntary payment of £19,637,950 to WealthTek clients and the FCA has censured the firm. The FCA found Sapia did not put enough safeguards in place to protect this money.

item.title: Sector Snapshot: EV Funding On Track For Modest Gains
item.description: The broad trend: Funding to EV-related startups reflects a similar mix of optimism and restraint. Yet funding remains far below prior peaks and exit activity appears muted. The numbers: Companies in Crunchbase’s electric vehicle category are on track to see higher investment this year relative to last.

item.title: Exclusive: Cloneable Raises $4.6M To ‘Clone’ Expert Worker Knowledge With Agentic AI For Utilities And Infrastructure
item.description: If we know what the expert is looking for, why can’t we just clone that expertise?” The startup’s founders realized that heavy industries — energy, oil and gas and agriculture — face a “knowledge crisis” as experienced workers retire faster than they can be replaced. Cloneable aims to capture and preserve that kind of institutional knowledge. Cloneable , a startup that uses AI to shadow human experts in heavy industries such as energy and replicate their specialized workflows into autonomous agents, has raised $4.6 million in seed funding, the company tells Crunchbase News exclusively.

item.title: Euler ILS Partners sees $1bn fund opportunity for data centre sidecar risk: Bloomberg
item.description: Euler ILS Partners, the specialist Swiss insurance-linked securities (ILS) investment manager, sees a $1 billion fund opportunity based on quota share’s to support insurance risk transfer capacity for the data centre build-out, according to reporting from Bloomberg. The publisher has interviewed Niklaus Hilti, Chief Investment Officer of Euler ILS Partners and said the company is targeting launch of a fund dedicated to assuming risk from data centres, while the goal will be to deliver double-digit returns to investors. Hilti told Bloomberg that Euler ILS Partners is planning to partner with an insurer to underwrite the specialist policies covering elements of risk related to data centres.

item.title: Regional hurricane landfall patterns, not basin-wide totals, key for 2026 ILS portfolios: Hohermuth, Schroders Capital
item.description: Benjamin Hohermuth of Schroders Capital ILS suggests that for insurance-linked securities (ILS) portfolios, the regional message is far more important than basin-wide totals. In a new article authored by Hohermuth, who serves as a Senior Nat Cat Specialist, Non-Life at Schroders Capital ILS, the specialist shared the firm’s outlook for the insurance-linked securities market as the key 2026 Atlantic hurricane season approaches. “For an ILS portfolio, the regional landfall pattern matters more than basin-wide landfalls.

item.title: Global property insurance rates fall 9% in Q1 2026, as appetites broaden: Marsh Risk
item.description: Broker Marsh Risk has revealed that property rates declined by 9% globally in the opening quarter of 2026, repeating the same trend seen in Q4 2025, with double-digit decreases recorded in major regions such as the Pacific, the United States and the United Kingdom. In its latest report on global commercial insurance rates, Marsh Risk highlighted that the downward rate movement in Q1’26 was supported by favourable reinsurance terms and a significant capacity oversupply. Commenting on the report, John Donnelly, President, Global Placement, Marsh Risk, said: “While the Middle East conflict is being carefully observed for its potential impact on insurance markets, the current competitive environment is expected to persist as insurer profitability remains strong.

item.title: Kingstone Companies extends Golden’s term as President and CEO till January, 2029
item.description: The Board of Directors of property and casualty (P&C) insurance holding company, Kingstone Companies, Inc., has extended Meryl Golden’s term as President and Chief Executive Officer through January 10th, 2029. Thomas Newgarden, Chairman of the Board, Kingstone Companies, commented, “This extension follows a review by the Compensation Committee and reflects the Board’s confidence in Ms Golden and in the plan she is executing. The Compensation Committee recommended this extension, as Golden’s current agreement was set to expire on January 10th, 2027.

item.title: Cyber insurance market enters critical phase amid softening rates and rising exposure: DUAL
item.description: Richard Clapham, CEO of DUAL, has warned that the global cyber insurance market is approaching a “significant inflection point”, as sustained softening, rising exposures and an increasingly elevated threat landscape converge to test long-term market sustainability. In a recent report, DUAL said cyber insurance has reached a critical juncture. “Cyber insurance has moved rapidly through distinct phases: early exposure-led growth, a sharp pricing correction and, more recently, sustained softening.

item.title: BMA and Lloyd’s introduce streamlined Market Entry pathway for insurance innovation
item.description: The Bermuda Monetary Authority (BMA), the regulator responsible for Bermuda’s financial services sector including insurance supervision, has introduced a new Market Entry & Innovation Pathway intended to support firms with established solutions in progressing more efficiently into the Bermuda market. The pathway is open to companies that have participated in, or graduated from, the Lloyd’s Lab programme and whose solutions are relevant to Bermuda’s insurance environment. Gerald Gakundi, Head of Insurance and Funds and Deputy Managing Director at the BMA, commented: “This streamlined process strengthens Bermuda’s position as a jurisdiction where innovation can thrive by combining regulatory clarity with global connectivity.

item.title: Beazley Shareholders Approve Zurich’s $10.9 Billion Cash Takeover
item.description: London-listed specialty insurer Beazley said on Wednesday shareholders approved Swiss group Zurich Insurance’s 8.1 billion pound ($10.94 billion) all-cash takeover, with 99.9% votes in favor at a meeting held earlier in the day. Earlier in March, Zurich raised 3.9 billion Swiss francs ($4.98 billion) in a share sale to help finance the Beazley acquisition , after the British insurer accepted an improved offer of up to 1,335 pence per share. Days after Zurich struck the deal with Beazley, it also agreed to buy Generali’s Irish P&C operations for 337 million euros ($394.69 million).

item.title: PwC Pays $166 Million to Settle HK Evergrande Audit Probe
item.description: PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP agreed to pay HK$1.3 billion ($166 million) in fines and compensation to settle investigations into its auditing work for the collapsed property giant China Evergrande Group. The measures come as the firm attempts to rebuild in the wake of Beijing’s earlier record fine over its audit work on China Evergrande. PwC was Evergrande’s auditor for more than a decade until it resigned in January 2023, due to what the developer said were audit-related disagreements.

item.title: Ex-Credit Suisse Team Plans $1 Billion Fund for Data Center Insurance Risk
item.description: A team of former Credit Suisse bankers is preparing to launch a fund they say will offer double-digit returns in exchange for taking on some of the insurance risk tied to data centers. It would be the first time a product in the market for insurance-linked securities specifically targets data-center risk, according to Hilti. Euler ILS Partners , an alternative investor formed through a 2024 management buyout of Credit Suisse Insurance Linked Strategies Ltd., is in the process of teaming up with an insurance company to underwrite specialist policies, Chief Investment Officer Niklaus Hilti told Bloomberg.

item.title: Simply Business has launched a new business insurance app within ChatGPT
item.description: Simply Business has launched a new business insurance app within ChatGPT in the U.S., alongside a separate rollout of a UK version, marking a further step in its digital distribution strategy. By embedding pricing capabilities into ChatGPT, Simply Business is aiming to simplify early-stage insurance discovery while maintaining a secure and structured path to full policy purchase through its platform. and the UK for small businesses in ChatGPT is our latest step in meeting our customers where they are and making the insurance-buying process an easier, better and fairer experience for them.” Dana Edwards, Group CTO at Simply Business, added: “Small business owners are already using platforms like ChatGPT to research, plan and make decisions.

item.title: Berkley errs on the side of being cautious and cheap
item.description: But as we’ve shared, we tend to err on the side of being cautious and cheap. Berkley reported a record quarter with operating EPS of $1.30, beating expectations by 14%, alongside a 21.2% ROE and $515 million in net income. But again, you never know what tomorrow will bring, but there’s a reason why we have not been historically active on that front.” Bottom Line: They dont need Berkley One, they need One Berkley.

item.title: Simply Business launches ChatGPT app
item.description: Simply Business launched a business insurance app in ChatGPT in the U.S., with a separate version for the UK. and the UK for small businesses in ChatGPT is our latest step in meeting our customers where they are and making the insurance-buying process an easier, better and fairer experience for them.” – David Summers, Group CEO at Simply Business. “Small business owners are already using platforms like ChatGPT to research, plan and make decisions.

item.title: Neptune leans into AI to create “super agents”
item.description: The company’s goal is clear: turn traditional agents into “super agents” capable of selling more efficiently and consistently. “Last quarter, I spoke about turning agents into what we call super agents. Neptune Flood is leaning into AI across its platform, aiming to reshape how flood insurance is sold while expanding its reach in an underinsured market.

item.title: Jochen Wilmes se une a Miller Spain para liderar el área de riesgos financieros
item.description: Miller Spain ha reforzado su posicionamiento en el segmento de grandes cuentas y riesgos complejos con la incorporación de Jochen Wilmes como director de Caución, Crédito y Riesgos. Miller Spain enmarca este nombramiento en su plan estratégico de crecimiento , que incluye el refuerzo de equipos y la especialización, con la incorporación reciente de Dina Urbano al área de Crédito y Caución y el apoyo de María Jesús Lainez y el resto del equipo. Con una sólida trayectoria en el ámbito asegurador y financiero, está especializado en la implementación de soluciones de riesgo y en la estructuración de programas de gestión para compañías nacionales e internacionales.
